item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Otamatone wants to be a good roommate!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh my god they pay rent!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the upvote! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cutest SCP. +1 because I can´t give it a +10.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love her 😭

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Japan makes some pretty cute musical instruments. It's crazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…what
The mini is even more adorable than the first one.
And the first one was freaking adorable already.
How.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted because I WANT ONE! AAAAAAAA

waaaaaah

Thank you for upvote!

(Good to see there's still room for whimsy here. Also that the Foundation hasn't got a damn clue about it.)

I'm glad to see people like whimsy! And yeah, the Foundation doesn't really know what's going on. But they're rolling with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted because I WANT ONE! AAAAAAAA

(Good to see there's still room for whimsy here. Also that the Foundation hasn't got a damn clue about it.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much for the kind comment! I'm glad to know that you felt this way after reading the article. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every once in a while, I read an SCP article that makes me wish it was real because it's the kind of anomaly that would just make the world a tiny bit better by existing. This is one of them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if there are any local/chain shops that sell these, but they're pretty widely available online.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, this will make me respect my next otamatone purchase! If only I knew where they sold these things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amazon sells them!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is cute and I want one.

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded:
Waaah! 😃

Also, fitting that the above comment was post number 83. Looks like a happy face.
Wahhhhh! :^)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't care for this thing one bit.

No worries; your taste is your taste.

Based on the description, this feels more like a dog trapped in a otomawhatevers body.

There are a number of behaviors/responses in the description that are not similar to the way dogs act.

This is just nice and cutesy for the sake of being nice and cutesy which sucks out any potential that this has

Out of curiosity, potential for what?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can't care for this thing one bit. Based on the description, this feels more like a dog trapped in a otomawhatevers body. This is just nice and cutesy for the sake of being nice and cutesy which sucks out any potential that this has

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh lol it's fine nbd yo

item.title: Re: I fluffing love these things.
item.content:encoded:
I wish my otamatones could help me find lost stuff… ;-;

Maybe you can ask them if they have some friends looking for a roommate. d:

But they do have a bed, so I'm close.

Aww, nice of you!

I don't have an otamatone, but I do have a lot of plants that my parents get as gifts and end up not having time (or interest in…) caring for. I make them little coasters to sit on. They haven't brought me any lost things though. Just spiders.

item.title: I fluffing love these things.
item.content:encoded:
I've been obsessed with these things for over 3 years, why did it take me so long to find this?

I wish my otamatones could help me find lost stuff… ;-;

But they do have a bed, so I'm close.

item.title: Re: Perfection
item.content:encoded:
Dawww, you're too kind. :3

I'm sure your otamatone appreciates you!

item.title: Perfection
item.content:encoded:
I love this. I love this fucking beautiful thing and everything about it. This is the best thing on the entire site. Truly, this is the epitome of cuteness.

I'm going to make sure my otamatone is nice and comfortable from now on. Maybe it will help me find lost things too ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad you think they're cute!

Otamatones can actually be ordered online.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very cute! :< I want one of this

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also thanks for the links to the oboe songs ^^

You're welcome! Happy listening. :D

As for the keter thing, it kinda falls into all 3 categories doesn't it. Safe because its not posing any threat- if anything it's helping everyone-

Ehhh… not really? Object classes are based on difficulty of containment, and in this case there are some specific criteria for having the anomaly manifest and keeping it localized to a certain location. Safe class objects can be essentially put in a locker without much else—in that same vein, an atomic bomb would be Safe class because while it technically would pose a threat if activated, it can just be locked up without having to fulfill care needs.

keter because it's uncontainable (or at least thats how I interpreted it). So I guess any classification works here, but I agree, safe seems most fitting.

It's not uncontainable because the Foundation has found a means to control the manifestation of the anomaly to a certain degree, and the anomalies are not actively causing damage/harm to humanity by trying to break containment (the roommates are pretty happy currently and aren't trying to leave). Also, I put in an earlier post "Definitely not Safe class, but definitely not Keter either." so uh…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What you said about the final fantasy music is pretty much exactly what I think about that song. I've never played any of the games, but that soundtrack pretty much just stands alone. Also thanks for the links to the oboe songs ^^
As for the keter thing, it kinda falls into all 3 categories doesn't it. Safe because its not posing any threat- if anything it's helping everyone- euclid because its sentient uh sapient, so it's inherently unpredictable, and keter because it's uncontainable (or at least thats how I interpreted it). So I guess any classification works here, but I agree, safe seems most fitting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You are much welcome, may your crops continue to flourish and your skin be clear as a sunny morning

(ﾉ◕ヮ◕)ﾉ*:・ﾟ✧*:・ﾟ✧*:・ﾟ✧ εїз εїз εїз

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: since reading this article my skin has cleared and my crops have flourished, thank you dearly for this blessing of cute

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well that brightened up a hard day at work to no end!

Glad to hear! I hope your day gets better!

I hope the Foundation lets Agent Kato's roommate and their kid stay.

They probably would. It's a pretty effective way to keep the anomaly contained, and also allows for further observation of said anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that brightened up a hard day at work to no end! I hope the Foundation lets Agent Kato's roommate and their kid stay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
an SCP It should not necessarily be about a deadly, uncontained or uncontrollable object, about a menace for the life, the sanity or the Space-Time Continuity for be interesting

This is basically everything I've written for the site ever, in a nutshell. I'm glad you enjoyed this piece!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the +1!

And yeah, this is something that people tend to stumble upon rather than go looking for. I think it's fitting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's the thing… judging by the rules this site made on its own, wouldn't this be keter?

It's not difficult to contain compared to some of the facility requirements for the big monster stuff, and it doesn't present any sort of inherent threat to humanity. I don't think it'd be Keter?

After all, it's unable to be contained, and actively breaks containment.

It's not the same Otamatone that appears in different apartments, and they're perfectly happy to continue renting with their roommate if the conditions are right. Definitely not Safe class, but definitely not Keter either.

ETA: (collapsed because orchestral music babbling)

+ show block
– hide block

They're the sort of thing that's impossible to take seriously, but when someone is really good at playing it, you don't really know if you should be laughing or crying.

BOTH. The Theme of Love is my favorite Final Fantasy music piece. It's absolutely gutpunching when you hear it by full orchestra: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IBL8Zyp7n4I and I tear up every goddamn time the main theme hits with all the other instruments.

The oboe is an underappreciated instrument really. It's used for the solo in the Theme of Love, as well as Across the Stars (for Anakin and Padme): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TfJ6wGKRtVs and also the opening of Han Solo and the Princess: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Hzldvp4kHTs

hello my name is zyn and i like orchestra music

that percussionist on cymbals was on point at 3:28

piccolo soloist man is top tier

i have a lot of feelings shhhh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It’s really adorable. Seriously, is nice read about an anomalous object that no is not a menace for anything, and reinforces the point that an SCP It should not necessarily be about a deadly, uncontained or uncontrollable object, about a menace for the life, the sanity or the Space-Time Continuity for be interesting. Take my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Otamatones are so weird! They're the sort of thing that's impossible to take seriously, but when someone is really good at playing it, you don't really know if you should be laughing or crying. But back to the skip, it's cute, weird, and whimsical, so +1. Super light compared to a lot of stuff on this site, so that made everything that much more strange.
Here's the thing… judging by the rules this site made on its own, wouldn't this be keter? After all, it's unable to be contained, and actively breaks containment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How odd that I barely stumbled upon this. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was intended: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-3172692/scp-3338#post-3561556

Reverted.

ETA: I was rereading this comment and I realized it can be read as kind of irritated. Sorry! I think I was in a hurry when I first posted it, but I just want to make it clear that there are no hard feelings here. ^^;;;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"おばちゃんワありがとう"
Isn't the ワ actually a は pronounced like "wa"?
I'll change it, but if this was entirely intended, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
waaaa (ﾉ◕ヮ◕)ﾉ*:・ﾟ✧*:・ﾟ✧

"what if coins found between the sofa cushions are just spiders paying rent"

Oh man, that's adorable! I have a couple spider tenants from time to time, but they earn their keep by eating mosquitoes and ants for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once a month, an SCP-3338 instance’s mouth will fill with Japanese yen paper money, totaling approximately 10 to 15 percent of the tenant’s monthly rent fee. Money generated by this method has been determined to be legal (non-counterfeit) currency

This reminds me of a picture I saw the other day of a cartoon spider holding up a single penny with a smile on its face, with the caption being "what if coins found between the sofa cushions are just spiders paying rent"

Other than that, this article was amazingly cute, all my +1s

waaaa

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww thanks! I'm glad you liked the cute Otamatone! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OH MY DAYS, that has got to be one of the cutest articles i've ever read, they're just so adorable!

+1, don't need to state why, it's just a great article in every way and thanks to this article i now know what a cute little Otamatone is!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everyone could use an Otamatone roommate!

Thank you for the +1. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, I dont think SCP's all have to be creepy, and this is neat and just strange and unique enough to grab my +1.
I love how it helps pay rent, I could use a couple, and they wouldnt leave dirty plates for me to clean up…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Every time I think the cute couldn’t possibly melt me any further… <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: :D I'm glad you like! And no worries, I'm happy you found this. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
YES! Zyn, you made this! And, d'awwwwwww, it's amazing!

I'm so so sorry it took me so long to find it, I only remembered that thread recently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would making another Otamatone SCP be unoriginal now?

Not necessarily! We have a fair number of articles about the same base object, like violins and airplanes and the like. It depends on how you approach the conceptualization of your otamatone article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh darn! I thought the same thing after seeing one! In fact, the way I stumbled upon this SCP was because I was searching for an appropriate picture for it, and I found yours! :P

Would making another Otamatone SCP be unoriginal now?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
otamatone wants to be roommate, not pet! omatatone will help pay rent!

Thanks for the d'aww and the +1/. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All SCPs need to provoke some kind of reaction, and most shoot for fear, or disgust, or sheer WTF. Many of my favorites, like Cassy, aim for d'aww.

Sentient otamatone-pets? D'aww. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do like lighthearted stuff! :) Thanks for the comment!

Take On Me on Otamatone is pretty cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a pretty cute skip here. It's good to see some lighthearted stuff on this site.

+1

also this gave me memories...

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww, thanks for the comment! I'm glad you liked the article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love seeing peaceful/friendly SCP's and this is one of the best i've seen, cute, simple and just all around pleasant. +1 for sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
waaaa

oh my goodness that split/screen duet with the otamatone with the little bow and the glasses is too cute help waaaaaaaa

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Waaaaaaaaaah ;u;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eeeeeeeee thank you for the vote and yay +100!

Wah :D
